thread.title: ICC Issues Arrest Warrants for Israeli Leaders Netanyahu and Gallant, Accuses Them of War Crimes
thread.title_full: ICC Issues Arrest Warrants for Israeli Leaders Netanyahu and Gallant, Accuses Them of War Crimes

author: time news
title: ICC Issues Arrest Warrants for Israeli Leaders Netanyahu and Gallant, Accuses Them of War Crimes
text: The ‚ÄćShort Version The International Criminal Court (ICC) has issued arrest warrants for‚Ā§ Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, former Defense Minister Yoav Gallant, and a Hamas leader. The ICC‚ÄĆ accuses‚ĀĘ them of‚ĀĘ war crimes, including using hunger as a weapon. Israel, the USA, and Qatar are not members of the ICC, but Norway is. Show more ‚Äď The ICC‚Äôs arrest warrant‚Ā§ for Israeli ‚ÄĆleaders is outrageous. Let me be clear: No matter what the ICC‚Äć may ‚Äćimply, there is no comparison between‚ÄĆ Israel and Hamas. We will always support Israel against threats to the country‚Äôs security, says President Joe Biden in‚Ā§ a statement. The International Criminal Court (ICC) prosecutes individuals accused of war crimes, crimes against‚Ā§ humanity, genocide, and aggression. Now, the court, which is based in The Hague, Netherlands, has ‚Äčissued an arrest warrant for Israeli ‚ÄčPrime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu,‚Ā§ former‚Ā§ Defense Minister Yoav Gallant, and Hamas leader AL-Masri, who is likely‚ĀĘ deceased. ‚Äď The ICC plays ‚ÄĆan important role in‚ĀĘ ensuring accountability for serious crimes.‚Ā§ It‚Ā§ is crucial that the ICC fulfills its mandate effectively. I trust that the court will handle the case further‚ÄĆ based on fundamental guarantees of due process, says‚ÄĆ Foreign Minister ‚Ā£Espen Barth Eide to VG. EU member states must respect the arrest warrant, emphasizes the‚Äč EU‚Äôs foreign chief during‚Äć a visit to Jordan. ‚Äď This is not a political decision. It is a decision made by an international criminal court. The decision must‚Ā£ be respected and implemented, says Borrell. When the ICC first hinted at issuing arrest warrants‚Ā§ in May, Eide stated that Norway‚Ā§ is obligated to ‚ĀĘarrest anyone with an arrest warrant if they come to‚Ā£ the country. ‚Äď Any person who visits‚Ā£ Norway and who has an arrest warrant issued by ‚Ā£the ICC will have to expect to‚Äć be ‚Äčhanded over‚Äč to the ICC, in accordance ‚ĀĘwith Norway‚Äôs obligations, Eide told Nettavisen. Israel‚Äôs Foreign‚Ā£ Minister Gideon Sa‚Äôar says that the ICC has ‚Äúlost all legitimacy‚ÄĚ with an ‚Äúabsurd‚ÄĚ decision, according to the news agency Reuters. ‚Äď The arrest warrants are important steps toward justice, ‚Ā§but are ‚ĀĘlimited if not followed ‚Äčup with action, says ‚ÄćHamas spokesperson Basem Naim, according to the agency. ‚Äď We completely reject the false and absurd accusations from the International Criminal Court, a ‚Äčbiased and discriminatory political body, says Netanyahu‚Äôs press officer, David Mencer, to TV 2 . What is the ICC? The International Criminal Court ‚ÄĆ(ICC) was established in 2002 and is the world‚Äôs first permanent‚Äć international court that prosecutes individuals‚ÄĆ accused of ‚Äčwar crimes, crimes against humanity, genocide, and aggression. The court is ‚ÄĆbased in The Hague, Netherlands. British Karim Khan is the chief prosecutor. 124 countries and territories are members, but not countries like the USA, Russia, China, India, and Israel. Since its inception, 31 cases have been tried at the ICC, resulting in ten convictions and ‚Äčfour acquittals. The‚ÄĆ ICC has issued ‚ĀĘarrest warrants for around 40 individuals over‚ĀĘ the years. Five men have been found guilty of war ‚Ā§crimes, ‚Ā£all of ‚Ā£whom are African militia ‚Ā£leaders. Sentences have varied from 9 to 30 ‚ĀĘyears in prison. The ICC should not be confused with the UN‚Äôs highest‚Äč judicial body, the International Court of Justice (ICJ), which deals with legal disputes between states. On‚Ā§ November 21, 2024, the ICC issued arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu,‚Äć Defense Minister Yoav Gallant, and the likely deceased Hamas leader‚Ā£ Mohammed al-Masri, also known as Mohammed Deif. The ICC‚Äôs prosecution requested such an arrest warrant already on ‚ÄćMay 20, 2024. The crimes are also said to include murder, persecution, and other inhumane acts, writes Reuters. What does this mean? One ‚Äćconsequence could be that Netanyahu and Gallant are arrested if they set foot in one of the ICC‚Äôs 124 member states. Israel, the ‚Ā£USA, and Qatar ‚Ā£are not members, unlike Norway. This ‚ĀĘdid not happen when Sudan‚Äôs former president Omar Hassan al-Bashir‚ÄĆ visited South Africa in 2015. They ‚Ā£let him‚Ā£ go, despite an outstanding arrest warrant from the ICC, writes The Guardian. The justification was that he was a sitting head of state. ‚Äď This shows ‚Äčthat we ‚Ā§have an international legal order that functions and does not take cold political considerations, but actually adheres to the rules that the international community has agreed upon, says legal advisor in Save the Children,‚ĀĘ Mads Harelm, to VG. ‚Äď The point here is that there should be no special treatment in this system. The ICC should adhere to the factual circumstances. Here, ‚ĀĘmany believe that we‚Äć are dealing with ‚ĀĘongoing violations of‚Äč international law, and ‚ĀĘthey have acted based ‚Äčon that, he says. ‚Äď I am glad the‚ÄĆ arrest warrant has come, says Terje Einarsen over the phone to VG. He lectures‚ÄĆ in international law at UiB and is the chair of ICJ Norway. This‚Ā£ summer, they submitted a written submission to the‚Ā£ court in The Hague. ‚Äď In my opinion,‚Ā£ it has been clear for a long time that Netanyahu and Gallant are responsible ‚ÄĆfor crimes in ‚Ā£Gaza. ‚Äď‚Ā§ Through the‚Äč combination ‚Äčof the‚ÄĆ blockade, extensive‚ĀĘ bombing, and ‚Äćeventually ‚Äčground invasion, along with the fact‚ĀĘ that the population in Gaza consists mainly of civilians ‚Äď this results in‚Ā§ actions that lead to violations of human rights. These characterize‚Ā£ crimes against humanity, he believes. The Charges The ICC states the‚Äč following‚ĀĘ in a statement about the kinds of crimes they believe Netanyahu and Gallant are behind. ‚Äď Reasonable grounds ‚Ā§to believe that both individuals willfully and knowingly deprived the civilian population in Gaza of items essential for‚ĀĘ their survival, including food, water, and medicines and medical supplies, as well as fuel and electricity, from at least October 8, 2023, to May 20, 2024. Furthermore, they state ‚Ā§that Israel has not done a good enough job for ‚ÄĆthe humanitarian assistance to ‚ÄĆGaza. And ‚Ā£that when‚ÄĆ they assisted with the humanitarian aid, it was not something Israel itself wanted. ‚Äď‚Ā£ The reality is that it was ‚Äća response to pressure from the international community or ‚Ā§requests from the USA. In any case, the increase in humanitarian assistance was not sufficient to improve ‚Ā£the population‚Äôs ‚ĀĘaccess to essential goods, says the‚Äč ICC. In May,‚Ā£ chief ‚Äčprosecutor Karim Khan at the ICC requested arrest warrants for ‚Ā£Netanyahu and several Hamas leaders. At that‚ĀĘ time, ‚Äčit was reported that a panel‚Ā§ of judges at the ICC had to consider whether to proceed. This has now been done. Hamas‚Äôs Military Leader An arrest warrant ‚Äćhas also been issued for Hamas leader Mohammed Diab Ibrahim Al-Masr (Deif). He is ‚Ā£the chief of the Al-Qassam brigade, which is Hamas‚Äôs military wing.
